title: sports-registry — field classification
description: Sports / Disciplines / Positions carry only reference data. Zero PII on any row. All three entities are safe to cache aggressively per-tenant + safe to expose in every consumer-scoped API response. The safeguarding-adjacent field is `gender_typical` — some sports have male-only or female-only governance rules (masters swimming M35 vs W35, most rugby unions), which downstream Athlete flows honour via the eligibility resolver. Bounds mutations to age_range_min/max on Sport can cascade to downstream AgeGroup + Team eligibility recomputations, so they audit at info severity.

regulated_minor: Sport.age_range_min (drives downstream AgeGroup eligibility filtering — a sport with age_range_min=6 refuses AgeGroups authoring rows for younger athletes)
Sport.gender_typical (some sports have governance-level gender segregation — downstream flows honour it via the eligibility resolver)
audit_log: Sport.slug (IMMUTABLE post-create; any attempted mutation is an audit-critical anomaly)
Sport.age_range_min (change fires SportUpdated with previous+new values; downstream AgeGroup eligibility may recompute)
Sport.gender_typical (change fires SportUpdated; may cascade to safeguarding flags on downstream rows)
Sport.is_active (deactivation removes the sport from tenant pickers; downstream historical enrollments still resolve via SportKeyResolver — audit at info)
Sport.is_platform_seeded (IMMUTABLE — any attempted flip is a system anomaly)
SportPrefixResolutionFailed events (an audit-critical signal — a downstream row references a sport_key/discipline_key/position_key that failed to resolve)

notes: Every row is REFERENCE data with zero PII. The catalog can be cached aggressively at every layer: browser (short TTL), CDN (medium TTL), Redis (long TTL), and in-process memoization for the request lifecycle.

The catalog IS the read-side authority — downstream analytics + BI queries may join against sports / disciplines / positions safely. Consumers never see other tenants' custom rows (the merged read scopes to platform + current tenant).

Bounds mutations on Sport.age_range_min/max are NOT full audit-critical (unlike AgeGroup.min_age_inclusive which drives per-athlete eligibility). They only advisory-shape the AgeGroup admin's UI. Downstream Athlete rows carry a snapshot of the operative AgeGroup at enrollment time, so Sport bound changes never invalidate enrollments retroactively.

`gender_typical` is a governance / advisory field — it shapes tenant UI (a female-only sport hides male-typical options in the picker) but does NOT enforce eligibility at the write path. Enforcement lives in downstream Athlete + Team modules.
Compliance retention on audit rows: 7 years for `SportPrefixResolutionFailed` (unresolved-reference signal — feeds compliance replay). 2 years for other Registry mutations.
